items.text:
Hi Jordan,

Your spring meal plan ended with a balance of $18.40. Please stop by the Dining Services Office in Reynolds Hall before May 30 to claim your refund. Bring your student ID and a printout of this email so our staff can process the request quickly at the front desk.

Thanks,
Dining Services

questions.explanation: The email states the balance is $18.40.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about claiming the refund?

options.A: It must be done in person before the deadline.
options.B: It can be deposited online by the student.
options.C: It is forfeited automatically each summer.
options.D: It must be mailed by the office.

questions.explanation: The student must visit Reynolds Hall with their ID before May 30, indicating in-person collection.

items.text:
STUDENT THEATER PRESENTS: THE LIGHTHOUSE KEEPER

Two nights only — Friday, April 12 and Saturday, April 13 at 7:30 PM. Whitman Hall Stage. Tickets $5 at the door. Cash or campus card accepted. Doors open thirty minutes before curtain. Latecomers will be seated only during scene breaks for the comfort of other audience members.

questions.stem: Where is the show being performed?
questions.explanation: The flyer states the performance is at Whitman Hall Stage.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about ticket purchases?

options.A: They are reserved only for faculty members.
options.B: They are available without advance booking.
options.D: They are limited to theater majors only.

questions.explanation: Tickets are sold $5 at the door, indicating no advance booking is necessary.

items.text: NOTICE: The water fountains on the third floor of Bishop Hall will be out of service from Monday, March 10 through Wednesday, March 12 for scheduled maintenance. Please use the second-floor fountains during this period. A staff member will be available to assist students with bottled water needs at the third-floor reception desk between classes.

questions.stem: Which floor's fountains are being repaired?
questions.explanation: The notice specifies the third-floor fountains will be out of service.

questions.stem: What should students on the third floor do for water?

options.A: Walk one floor down to find water.
options.B: Wait until evening for restored service.
options.D: Report any leaks to facilities staff.

questions.explanation: Students are advised to use the second-floor fountains during the outage.

items.text: NOTICE: Campus Wi-Fi will undergo maintenance on Sunday, April 21 from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM. Wired connections in Anderson Hall remain available throughout the window. A $5 lab pass covers visitor printing during the outage at the help desk. Students should save any active work before the maintenance window begins on Sunday morning.

questions.stem: How long will the maintenance last?
questions.explanation: Maintenance runs from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM, which is four hours.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Anderson Hall during the outage?

options.A: It will be closed to students overnight.
options.B: It will only allow faculty access.
options.C: It will require a wireless password.
options.D: It will still allow internet use through cables.

questions.explanation: Wired connections in Anderson Hall remain available, implying cabled internet access continues.
